title: Fix queue slice LHS assignment being silently discarded
body:
## Summary

This patch fixes queue slice assignment on LHS (`q[a:b] = rhs`) which was
silently discarded at runtime. The slice was lowered to `VlQueue::slice()`
which returns a temporary copy, so the assignment wrote to the copy and the
original queue was never modified.

## Reproducer

```systemverilog
module t;
  initial begin
    int q[$] = '{10, 20, 30, 40, 50};
    q[1:3] = '{99, 88, 77};
    // Expected: q = '{10, 99, 88, 77, 50}
    // Actual:   q = '{10, 20, 30, 40, 50} (unchanged)
    if (q[1] != 99) $stop;
    $write("*-* All Finished *-*\n");
    $finish;
  end
endmodule
```

On master:
```
%Error: t.v:7: Verilog $stop
```
(Compiles without error but assignment is silently discarded -- `q[1]` remains `20`)

## Changes

- Added `VlQueue::sliceAssign(lsb, msb, src)` and back-index variants
  to `include/verilated_types.h` -- assigns `src` elements to `q[lsb:msb]`
  in place with bounds clamping
- Added `DYN_SLICE_ASSIGN`, `DYN_SLICE_ASSIGN_FRONT_BACK`,
  `DYN_SLICE_ASSIGN_BACK_BACK` enum values in `src/V3AstAttr.h`
- In `visit(AstNodeAssign*)` (`src/V3Width.cpp`), detect LHS `CMethodHard`
  slice calls and rewrite `Assign(q.slice(a,b), rhs)` to
  `q.sliceAssign(a, b, rhs)`

## Test

- Added `test_regress/t/t_queue_slice_assign.{v,py}` -- middle, start,
  end, and single-element slice assignments with value and size checks

---
Developed by PlanV GmbH, assisted with Claude Code.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
